chunks.title: Abbey & Mayor — Per-Player Components
chunks.content:
## Each Player Receives (in their color, held in personal supply)

| Piece | Qty | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Abbey tile | 1 | Placed **instead** of drawing a tile; held all game |
| Mayor figure | 1 | New city-follower type |
| Wagon figure | 1 | Can occupy road, city, or monastery |
| Barn figure | 1 | Placed at field corners |

> With fewer than 6 players, return unused colors' pieces to the box.

**12 new land tiles** are shuffled into the regular tile supply. **Abbey tiles are NOT shuffled in** — each player holds their own.

chunks.title: Abbey & Mayor — Abbey, Mayor, Wagon, Barn
chunks.content:
## New Pieces Quick Reference

| Piece | Placement | Key Effects |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| **Abbey** | Instead of drawing a tile; fill a gap (tiles on all 4 orthogonal sides); no edge-matching required | Terminates all adjacent features. Scored like a monastery: 9 pts complete, 1 pt/surrounding tile incomplete. Monk may be placed on it. |
| **Mayor** | In a city, during follower deployment step | Majority strength = number of pennants in that city. If 0 pennants → Mayor counts as 0 followers (scores nothing). Returned when city scored. |
| **Wagon** | Road, city, or monastery (not fields), during follower deployment step | When its feature is scored, may move to an adjacent incomplete unoccupied feature instead of returning to supply. |
| **Barn** | At a corner point where 4 field quadrants converge (between tiles, not on a tile feature); may be placed even if farmers are present | On placement: all farmers in that field are immediately scored (3 pts/completed adjacent city, normal majority) and returned. No new farmers or barns may join that field afterward. End-game: most barns in field = 4 pts/completed adjacent city (ties share). |

### Mayor — Pennant Majority Table

| City Pennants | Mayor Counts As |
|---|---|
| 0 | 0 followers (scores nothing) |
| 1 | 1 follower (ties a lone Knight) |
| 2 | 2 followers (beats lone Knights) |
| 3+ | 3+ followers |

### Wagon Adjacency

"Adjacent" = features sharing a tile edge or road junction with the just-scored feature. The Wagon cannot move to a completed or already-occupied feature.

chunks.title: Abbey & Mayor — Step 1: Abbey Option
chunks.content:
## Step 1 Change: Placing Your Abbey

Instead of drawing a tile from the stack, you may place your **Abbey tile** on your turn.

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| When | Only if you still have your Abbey tile (one per player, used once) |
| Where | In a gap — an empty space with tiles on **all 4 orthogonal sides** |
| Edge-matching | **Not required** — the Abbey ignores normal edge-matching rules |
| Draw a tile too? | No — placing the Abbey fully replaces your draw |

After placing the Abbey, proceed normally to Step 2 (you may deploy a follower on it as a Monk).

chunks.title: Abbey & Mayor — Step 2: New Figure Options
chunks.content:
## Step 2: Additional Deployment Options

In addition to a normal follower, you may deploy one of the following:

| Figure | Where to Deploy | Special Rule |
|---|---|---|
| **Mayor** | City only | Counts as followers = number of **pennants** in that city (0 if no pennants) |
| **Wagon** | Road, city, or monastery | After the feature is **scored**, Wagon may move to an adjacent unoccupied incomplete feature |
| **Barn** | Field corner where **4 field quadrants meet** | On placement, **immediately score and return** all farmers in that field |
| **Monk** | Abbey tile (if Abbey placed in Step 1) | Functions like a Monk on a monastery |

> You still deploy **at most one figure** in Step 2.

chunks.title: Abbey & Mayor — Barn & Farm Scoring
chunks.content:
## Barn & Farm Scoring

### On Barn Placement (immediate)

| Who Scores | Rate | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| Player(s) with majority of farmers in that field | 3 pts per completed adjacent city | Normal farmer majority rules apply |

Farmers are returned to their owners after this scoring. No more farmers may join the field afterward.

### End-Game Farm Scoring with Barns

| Owner | Rate | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| Player with most barns in a field | 4 pts per completed adjacent city | Majority of barns wins |
| Tied barn counts | 4 pts each per completed adjacent city | Ties share |
| Minority barn holders | 0 pts | — |

### Key Differences vs Base Game

| Rule | Base Game | With Barn |
|---|---|---|
| End-game rate | 3 pts/completed city | **4 pts/completed city** |
| Farmers scored at end-game? | Yes (majority) | No — already returned at barn placement |
| Majority metric at end-game | Follower count | Barn count |

chunks.title: Abbey & Mayor — Mayor City Scoring
chunks.content:
## Mayor City Scoring

The Mayor's follower-count equals the number of **pennants** in the city it occupies.

| City Pennants | Mayor Counts As | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 0 followers | Mayor scores **0 pts** from that city |
| 1 | 1 follower | Ties a lone Knight |
| 2+ | 2+ followers | Beats lone Knights, wins majority |

- Majority math is otherwise unchanged: most followers = full pts, tied = both score full, minority = 0.
- A Mayor placed in a 0-pennant city yields no points — a key trap for new players.

## Abbey Scoring

Scored exactly like a monastery:

| Condition | Points |
|---|---|
| Completed (all 8 surrounding spaces filled) | 9 pts |
| Incomplete at end-game | 1 pt per surrounding tile including the Abbey (max 9) |
